records.description: deacon can auto-forward container ports to the host via a host-side daemon backed by a per-container registry and PID markers under the user-data folder — a capability the reference CLI does not model.

deacon resolves the full `extends` chain eagerly across read-configuration/up/build (merging base config, rejecting missing/cyclic targets), an ahead-of-spec capability tracking the in-flight proposal devcontainers/spec#22 that the reference CLI v0.87.0 does not implement. Recorded as an extension so the extends divergences are never misreported as parity drift.
deacon can inject the host's CA certificates into its TLS trust store (for OCI registry and network access), enabled via settings.json — a capability the reference CLI does not model.

deacon's --secrets-file auto-detects and accepts a .env-format KEY=VALUE file in addition to the flat JSON the reference CLI requires — a strict superset so an existing docker/compose .env drops in unchanged.
deacon applies user-defined profiles from settings.json (selected via --profile / DEACON_PROFILE) to layer default flags and configuration — a concept the reference CLI does not have.

deacon gates host-side lifecycle hooks (initializeCommand and future workspace-resident host hooks) behind an explicit workspace-trust opt-in before any host execution — a protection the upstream spec does not mandate.
